title: Shanghai 11/4/2024 — lost phone, anxious in class, dial the fuck
html:
Shanghai 11/4/2024 — lost phone, anxious in class, dial the fuck

Source: 2024-11-06 Google Doc. Tiny mid-Shanghai study abroad anxiety check-in. Phone is lost in an "immensely slim" recovery situation, baseline anxiety elevated, awareness as the only comfort.

TL;DR

  • The state: feeling more anxious than usual on average. "My awareness of it is comforting." — meta-cognition as the only available regulation tool.
  • The triggering loss: phone gone, traveled somewhere "very inconvenient" — chances of recovery slim.
  • The class drift: in class but not dialed in, watching the clock tick toward noon. Disengagement layered on top of anxiety.
  • The pragmatic resignation: "May just need to bite the bullet and get a new phone."
  • The closer (Yahir's standard self-instruction): "Keep cortisol levels down, dial the fuck//" — the imperative-form self-talk that recurs across his thoughts-and-things genre.

Pattern observed

This is one of the shorter, more vulnerable China chapter docs — not a manifesto, not a strategy note, just a quick anxiety waypoint with a "dial the fuck" closer. Compare to the longer reflective Shanghai docs from late November (e.g. 2024 11 27 thoughts and things shanghai 11272024).

Notable: the awareness-as-regulation move ("my awareness of it is comforting") is the same pattern that surfaces in the "have I made myself into a concept" doc — Yahir treats meta-cognition itself as the calming agent. Doesn't try to change the feeling; names the feeling and that's enough.

The lost-phone-in-China event is identity-anchoring — separation from primary device while abroad is one of the more disorienting modern travel experiences. The terse handling ("bite the bullet") shows Yahir defaulting to operational mode rather than spiraling.

Key quotes

  • "Ive been wanting to write that I have been on average feeling more anxious that usual, my awareness of it is comforting."
  • "Keep cortisol levels down, dial the fuck//"

Open questions

  • Did the phone ever turn up? (Per later Shanghai docs: Yahir got a new phone.)
  • What city in China did it travel to? (Doc doesn't say.)
  • The "//" terminator — Yahir's signature mid-thought interruption marker; recurs across docs.
